title: QMaxent: A QGIS plugin for Maxent species distribution modeling
description:
QMaxent is a QGIS plugin that integrates the elapid Python library to provide a reproducible, GIS-native Maxent species distribution modelling (SDM) environment. It supports model training, spatial projection, spatial cross-validation (Geographic K-Fold as the default; Checkerboard, Random K-Fold, Buffered Leave-One-Out), jackknife and permutation variable importance, export of QGIS-resident data to maxent.jar-compatible formats (SWD or ESRI ASCII Grid), and stratified priority-site selection for post-prediction field surveys — all within a single QGIS session. Dependencies are isolated in a plugin-private virtual environment that does not affect the host QGIS Python installation.

QMaxent (한국어)— QGIS 내부에서 Maxent 기반 종 분포 모델링(SDM)의 전 과정—자료 준비·래스터 정합·모델 적합·공간 교차검증·잭나이프·치환 변수 중요도·공간 투영·신규 조사지 우선순위 산정—을 수행하는 재현 가능한 GIS-내장 SDM 운영 환경입니다. elapid Python 라이브러리를 백엔드로 사용하며, 의존성은 시스템 QGIS에 영향을 주지 않는 플러그인 전용 가상환경에 격리 설치됩니다.

The companion manuscript will be cited here once accepted.
notes: An accompanying manuscript describes the design, the maxent.jar numerical-compatibility benchmark on the Pitta nympha dataset (Lee et al. 2025, Global Ecology and Conservation 60:e03939), and the SDM-to-survey closed-loop demonstration. The maxent.jar input-export feature enables GIS-native reproducibility audits of existing maxent.jar publications. Once that manuscript is accepted, its DOI will be added to related_identifiers and to the CITATION.cff preferred-citation block.
